notes: The current platform has one physical Hetzner host, so guest- or software-level warm standbys can be implemented but cross-host active redundancy cannot.
Any future R3 declaration remains a design target until a second failure domain exists and live apply is allowed to converge both domains.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore or rebuild monitoring when Alertmanager or the guest cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Replay the monitoring stack converge on monitoring after the restored guest is healthy.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore runtime-control when the API gateway container, config, or shared runtime data cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild runtime-control, then rerun the API gateway converge from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore backup from the off-site Proxmox copy when the local PBS VM or datastore is lost or corrupted.
standby.failback_method: Bring backup back from the off-site copy, confirm the datastore, then resume scheduled Proxmox backups.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when the browser-runner runtime, its Playwright image build, or its artifact path cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then rerun the browser-runner converge and smoke verification from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when the Changedetection runtime, its watch datastore, or the shared runtime state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then rerun the Changedetection converge and gateway verification workflow.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore or rebuild nginx when the published static portal or its edge files cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore nginx, regenerate the portal, and replay edge publication from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore coolify when the Coolify control plane or its managed runtime state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild coolify, then rerun the Coolify converge and smoke deployment verification from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore coolify-apps when the Coolify applications runtime or its managed proxy state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild coolify-apps, register it as the Coolify deployment server, then rerun the Coolify converge and repo-backed smoke deployment verification from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when the Crawl4AI runtime, its browser-backed crawl path, or the shared runtime state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then rerun the Crawl4AI converge and private guest-network verification workflow.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when Dify or its runtime state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then rerun the Dify converge from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime or postgres when Directus or the dedicated directus database cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime and postgres as needed, then rerun the Directus converge plus public token verification workflow.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-build when the build worker guest or its persistent workspace cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-build, then rerun the documented build-host converge and cache warm-up.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when the runtime VM or its shared service state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then replay the required service converges from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore or rebuild nginx when the published docs files or edge publication cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore nginx, regenerate the docs portal, and replay edge publication from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when Dozzle or the shared runtime state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then rerun the Dozzle converge from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when Excalidraw, the room backend, or the shared runtime state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, then rerun the Excalidraw converge from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime or the shared postgres leader when Flagsmith, its seeded control-plane metadata, or the authenticated edge publication path cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore the affected guest, confirm postgres leadership and OpenBao state, then rerun the Flagsmith converge and seeded-state verification from git.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore runtime-control when the Gitea runtime or its persistent state cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild runtime-control, then rerun the Gitea converge and runner reconnect workflow.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore docker-runtime when the GlitchTip runtime, DSN bootstrap state, or notification wiring cannot be recovered in place.
standby.failback_method: Restore or rebuild docker-runtime, rerun the GlitchTip converge from git, and repeat the public health plus synthetic event verification before resuming operator use.

standby.failover_trigger: Restore or rebuild monitoring when Grafana or the monitoring guest cannot be recovered in place.

standby.failover_trigger: No cold or warm standby exists; rebuild the host-local service from git and backups after host recovery.
standby.failback_method: Reapply the host-local Headscale automation on the rebuilt primary host.

standby.failover_trigger: Promote postgres-replica through the documented Patroni failover path when postgres or the current leader becomes unavailable.
standby.failback_method: Rebuild the old primary as a replica, let it resync, and only then move the writable VIP back if a planned failback is required.

proofs.trigger: Operator-initiated planned PostgreSQL failover rehearsal from ws-0188-live-apply.
proofs.observed_data_loss: No switchover occurred because the standby guest and VIP path were both unavailable, so no data-loss window was exercised.
proofs.health_verification: VM 151 is absent from `qm list`, `/etc/patroni` on VM 150 only contains template inputs, both `patroni` and `keepalived` are inactive on postgres, and the Proxmox host can open `10.10.10.50:5432` but not the expected VIP `10.10.10.55:5432`.
proofs.rollback_result: Not applicable because no promotion or failback was performed.

standby.failover_trigger: No cold or warm standby exists; restore host access after host recovery and Proxmox reinstall.
standby.failback_method: Reapply the host bootstrap and Proxmox access automation on the rebuilt primary host.
